name: ship-task
description: The mechanical tail of a one-PR-per-task loop, as a single command. After your review passes, ship pushes the current branch, opens a PR, waits for CI to reach a terminal state, squash-merges and deletes the branch, syncs the base, and optionally runs a post-merge command (e.g. `anvil apply`). Makes no review decisions and stops if CI fails — built to keep the repetitive git/gh/CI/merge dance out of an agent's context window.
